title: Monday 31st March 2025
description: As Trump’s Greenland talk masks climate concerns and oil giants pour $700mn into carbon storage, Europe sees lawsuits for climate inaction while the US shields against them. Greenhushing rises, but pension funds stand firm on ESG. In the UK, emissions take centre stage—literally—with the Kyoto climate deal adapted for theatre, while questions grow over gender gaps in active travel investment.
author: DailyGreen.News

title: Thursday 26th March 2025
description: Climate change is dropped from spy agency threat lists as ING becomes the first global bank with SBTi-approved financed emissions targets and OECD research shows climate action boosts growth. China’s glaciers have shrunk 26%, Indonesia secures $20bn to shut coal plants, and Airbus doubles down on hydrogen aircraft. In the UK, the Government faces a High Court deadline to revise its climate plan by Oct 2025, Dorset brings forward its net zero goal to 2035 with £6mn, and a shift to plant-based meals could save the NHS £54.9mn a year.
author: DailyGreen.News

title: Tuesday 11th March 2025
description: Try the MIT net zero simulator, US Supreme Court clears way for state climate suits, Mark Carney’s rise fuels Canadian climate hopes, and BP’s retreat from renewables raises banking questions—while the UK plans a jet fuel levy for SAF, Barrow eyes the UK’s largest floating solar farm, and councils struggle with net-zero targets.
author: DailyGreen.News

title: Friday 7th March 2025
description: Global sea ice hits record low, US exits coal-to-green funding deal, and Germany’s green jobs double amid staff shortages—while Microsoft inks 30-year India carbon removal deal, UK boosts floating offshore wind with £55.7M, and Europe's largest battery slashes energy bills.
author: DailyGreen.News

title: Monday 3rd March 2025
description: US Senate scraps methane fines, China misses their own climate target, and Wells Fargo drops net-zero financed emissions goals—while Goldman nears its $750B climate target, HSBC defends fossil fuels, and Labour’s green cuts spark criticism amid UK net-zero tensions. The turbines are going black and is Ed Miliband in for the chop?
author: DailyGreen.News

title: Thursday 20th February 2025
description: Greta Thunberg rejected. Biodegradable bottles boosted. HSBC massively delays net-zero targets, Amazon and Microsoft aim to fix carbon markets, and Trump fast-tracks fossil fuels—while BP investors push for climate votes, UK heat pump sales surge 67%, and the EU mandates clothing brands to pay for recycling.
author: DailyGreen.News

title: Wednesday 12th February 2025
description: UK announces £300mn for Active Travel. UK only industrial nation on track for Paris Agreement targets. SEC drops climate disclosure rule, Australian banks exit Net-Zero Alliance, and BP shifts focus from climate to shareholder returns—while Singapore sets new emissions targets, Shell faces Dutch Supreme Court, and Saudi plans a $5bn net-zero AI data centre.
author: DailyGreen.News

title: Wednesday 5th February 2025
description: January Climate Shocks: Global temperature record set, 2oC target declared ‘dead,’ and energy transition investment hits $2.1T—while Trump purges climate data, Malaysia drafts climate bill, and the UK unveils £100M seed fund alongside £250M for flood defences in a changing world.
author: DailyGreen.News

title: Monday 3rd February 2025
description: Trump’s Treasury exits climate network, USDA scrubs climate mentions, and Microsoft secures 7M+ tonnes in carbon removal—while Heathrow runway faces scrutiny, Scottish court blocks North Sea oil, and fire chiefs warn Britain isn’t ready for climate crisis impacts. All whilst the rats benefit.
author: DailyGreen.News

title: Friday 24th January 2025
description: Standing Firm Edition: Bloomberg pledges $20M for UN climate budget, Delta reaffirms climate goals, and Switzerland mandates net-zero for all businesses—while Heathrow expansion sparks UK debate, Microsoft signs CO2 forest deal, and Allianz & EIB raise €450M for emerging markets climate fund.
author: DailyGreen.News

title: Tuesday 21st January 2025
description: Trump's Day 1 Decisions Dominate: Paris exit, energy emergency, and green rollbacks lead climate headlines—while Japan invests $70B in carbon credits, Amazon electrifies logistics, and Indonesia opens carbon markets, as global efforts clash with Trump’s sweeping climate policy reversals.
author: DailyGreen.News

title: Monday 20th January 2025
description: Trump Inauguration Edition: US poised to exit Paris Agreement as Trump plans climate policy rollbacks, Fed withdraws from global climate group, and Canada’s biggest banks leave Net-Zero Alliance—while tech giants adjust, NYC’s climate lawsuit is dismissed, and extreme weather costs Canada a record $8.5B.
author: DailyGreen.News

title: Friday 17th January 2025
description: NY commits $1B to green economy, Google inks record biochar deal, and UK invests £410M in fusion energy—while Australia faces uninsurability, BP cuts jobs over green strategy, and OnPath Energy pledges £1B to accelerate UK wind and solar growth.
author: DailyGreen.News

title: Wednesday 15th January 2025
description: Biden locks in $74bn for climate funding, Apollo & Standard Chartered unveil $3bn transition fund, and Meta partners with Engie for Texas solar—while US bank exits threaten Southeast Asia, UK signs minerals deal with Saudi Arabia, and Amazon orders record 148 electric HGVs.
author: DailyGreen.News

title: Tuesday 14th January 2025
description: Net Zero Asset Managers suspends activities after BlackRock exit, NHS launches £500M net-zero framework, and Sunfire raises €200M for hydrogen tech—while ESG risks face stricter oversight, Barclays sees sustainability reshuffle, and Labour's renewable push sparks China debate.
author: DailyGreen.News

title: Wednesday 8th January 2025
description: Net-Zero Banking Alliance unravels as all major US banks exit, Biden bans offshore drilling, Germany cuts emissions 48% since 1990, and UK hits record clean electricity—while climate disasters, green pledges, and billion-dollar wind projects shape 2024’s net-zero narrative.
author: DailyGreen.News
